chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI Crl. Misc. Appl. No.12 of 2024 (Tahir Khan v. SSP Complaint Cell and others) __________________________________________________________ Date Order with signature of Judge __________________________________________________________ 1. For orders on MA No.341/2024 2. For hearing of main case 29.05.2024 Mr. Raheel Ali Bhatti, advocate for the applicant ========= 1. Granted subject to all just exceptions. 2. The applicant based on certain allegations by making an application u/s 22-A/B Cr.PC sought the direction against SHO PS PIB Colony Karachi to record his FIR; it was dismissed by learned VIth- Additional Sessions Judge / Ex-Officio Justice of Peace Karachi East vide order dated 28.12.2023, which is impugned by the applicant before this Court by way of the instant Crl. Misc. Application. It is contended by learned counsel for the applicant that a cognizable offence has taken place, therefore, learned Ex-Officio Justice of Peace ought not to have dismissed the application of the applicant by way of the impugned order; the same being illegal is to be examined by this Court. Heard arguments and perused the record. If for the sake of arguments, it is believed that the incident as alleged by the applicant has taken place and for that his FIR is not being recorded by the police then he has an alternate remedy to exhaust by filing a complaint of the alleged incident in terms of 200 Cr.PC before the Court having jurisdiction; such remedy if exhausted by him besides being alternate would be adequate in the circumstance of the case. No illegality even otherwise is noticed in the impugned order which may justify this Court to interfere with the same.

If for the sake of arguments, it is believed that the incident as alleged by the applicant has taken place and for that his FIR is not being recorded by the police then he has an alternate remedy to exhaust by filing a complaint of the alleged incident in terms of 200 Cr.PC before the Court having jurisdiction; such remedy if exhausted by him besides being alternate would be adequate in the circumstance of the case. No illegality even otherwise is noticed in the impugned order which may justify this Court to interfere with the same. Consequent to the above discussion, the instant Crl. Misc. Application is dismissed in limine. J U D G E Nadir*
